creator: se@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Tatsuki Makino from comment #14)
> But I also think this is not a Ports infrastructure problem.

It is a ports infrastructure problem for the reasons explained before.
While it is easily possible to treat RUN_DEPENDS like BUILD_DEPENDS and require all RUN_DEPENDS to be built and installed before installing the port that needs them, this would lead to dependency loops.

It is trivial to add a dependency required during the build to BUILD_DEPENDS, but it is very hard (or impossible) to work around dependency loops.

If you want to have BUILD_DEPENDS be treated like run depends, this is a trivial change in portmaster. But please do not open PRs due to dependency loops, then.

> Because portmaster appends -DNO_DEPENDS to each target extract,patch,stage,install and runs make.

Yes, of course. Portmaster provides a much more complete logic for dependency handling than the ports system alone. Ports may specify an acceptable version range for dependencies, but the ports system will not update already installed but outdated dependencies, if they fall into the range that is accepted.

That causes issues in a few ports that depend on each other and where the dependencies must be built first for a working system. (This could apply to dovecot and dovecot-pigeonhole, for example, but I have not tested building them in the "wrong" order, only observed that rebuilding dovecot alone will cause the service to fail.)

> I forget what was wrong with portmaster, but there is an option -t that changes its behavior.

No, the -t option exists for a different reason and it has shown not to solve the missing BUILD_DEPENDS case.

With -t dependencies of dependencies are recursively checked and updated if required. If you have a port A that has a run dependency on port B which needs shared libraries from port C, then  deleting port C from the system will cause port B to break. (This should never happen, unless port C is deleted with "pkg delete -f", a "pkg delete C" would cause A and B to be deleted as dependencies that cannot be run without C.)

In this example, the -t option would find that port C is missing and would add it to the list of ports to be installed if "portmaster -t A" is invoked. Since this recursive search for dependencies adds complexity and thus run-time, it is not the default, and it is only required in the scenario outlined above, where required run dependencies have been forcefully removed from the system.


I had offered to provide a patch that makes BUILD_DEPENDS perform like RUN_DEPENDS, before. This is the wrong fix, and I'll ignore any PRs due to dependency loops caused by such a change.

You have provided the definition of BUILD_DEPENDS that is required to build "japanese/xpdf" in comment 10. But that definition should have been added to that port's Makefile, not to /etc/make.conf (where it needs to be made conditional).

If you had opened a PR for japanese/xpdf and requested that the BUILD_DEPENDS line was added to that port, the issue would have been fixed for everybody instead of locally for you.
